name: Leek-And-Artichoke Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Water, acidulated with the juice of 1 Lemon
2 medium Leeks, white part only (washed and sliced)
1 small New potato, quartered and thinly sliced
6 Leaves butter lettuce (sliced into 1/4 inch strips)
a handful of Sorrel leaves, sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the artichokes by removing the tough outer leaves and slicing off the top 2/3 of the remaining inner leaves.
2. Cut the artichokes into quarters and remove the choke.
3. Dice the trimmed hearts into small pieces. Place them in the acidulated water as you work.
4. Heat the olive oil in a large pot, then add the leeks, drained artichoke quarters, whole garlic cloves, potato slices, lettuce or sorrel, parsley, and mint.
5. Season with salt and add 1/2 cup of water. Stir gently and simmer until the vegetables are wilted, about 5 minutes.
6. Add the peas (fresh or frozen) and the remaining water. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and partially cover the pot. Simmer for 20 minutes or until the artichokes are cooked through.
7. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
8. Serve hot, garnished with freshly ground pepper, a few pinches of chopped chervil or parsley, a drizzle of olive oil in each serving dish, and a dusting of grated Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese.
